content.text:
<user_query>
Explore the Next.js project at /Users/rishikeshjoshi/v0-design-invoicing and find:

1. All database connection configs — look for Neon references vs Supabase. Check lib/db.ts, lib/neon.ts, any .env files, next.config.ts/js, package.json for @neondatabase.
2. How the dashboard layout and nav works — read app/dashboard/layout.tsx or any layout file, check what nav component is used and how routing is done (look for Link vs router.push).
3. How invoice templates work — read app/dashboard/invoices/new/page.tsx and any invoice form components. Look for how invoice_templates or profile settings are saved. Check if there's an invoice template or settings page.
4. How invoices are created/saved — check app/api/invoices/route.ts or any POST route for invoices.
5. What the invoice PDF/print template looks like — look for components/invoice-template.tsx or similar.
6. Check components/bank-statement-upload.tsx for what it does currently.

Return the full content of: lib/db.ts, app/dashboard/layout.tsx (or the main layout), app/dashboard/invoices/new/page.tsx, and any invoice creation API route. Also report all Neon vs Supabase references found.
</user_query>

Exploring the project for database configs, dashboard layout, invoice flows, and related files.

[REDACTED]

input.final_summary: Searching the codebase for database configs, dashboard layout, invoice flows, and related files.
The app uses Supabase for auth and DB (not Neon at runtime). Dashboard nav uses Link; invoices save via POST /api/invoices; PDFs render via template-preview components.
